registry.description: Stubbornness is the refusal to be moved — the persistence of a chosen position or direction in the face of contrary evidence, wise counsel, or God's own appeal to change. The Hebrew vocabulary here captures the hardened, stiff quality: the stiff neck is the image of the person who refuses to turn. Stubbornness is different from faithful endurance: endurance holds to what is right under pressure, while stubbornness holds to what is wrong under appeal. Israel's stubbornness toward God is one of the central themes of the prophetic literature.

terms.medium_def: stubbornness, hardness, firmness

terms.medium_def:
1) to be hard, be severe, be fierce, be harsh
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to be hard, be difficult
1a2) to be hard, be severe
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to be ill-treated
1b2) to be hard pressed
1c) (Piel) to have severe labour (of women)
1d) (Hiphil)
1d1) to make difficult, make difficulty
1d2) to make severe, make burdensome
1d3) to make hard, make stiff, make stubborn
1d3a) of obstinacy (fig)
1d4) to show stubbornness

verses.esv_text: Deu 15:18 It shall not seem hard to you when you let him go free from you, for at half the cost of a hired worker he has served you six years . So the Lord your God will bless you in all that you do .

verses.esv_text: Deu 2:30 But Sihon the king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him, for the Lord your God hardened his spirit and made his heart obstinate , that he might give him into your hand , as he is this day .
verses.preview_html: Deu 2:30 But Sihon the king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him, for the Lord your God hardened his spirit and made his heart obstinate, that he might give him into your hand, as he is this day.

verses.esv_text: Exo 13:15 For when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go , the Lord killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt , both the firstborn of man and the firstborn of animals . Therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all the males that first open the womb , but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem .’
verses.preview_html: Exo 13:15 For when Pharaoh stubbornly refused to let us go, the Lord killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt, both the firstborn of man and the firstborn of animals. Therefore I sacrifice to the Lord all the males that first open the womb, but all the firstborn of my sons I redeem.’

verses.esv_text: Exo 7:3 But I will harden Pharaoh’s heart , and though I multiply my signs and wonders in the land of Egypt ,
verses.preview_html: Exo 7:3 But I will harden Pharaoh’s heart, and though I multiply my signs and wonders in the land of Egypt,

verses.esv_text: Gen 35:16 Then they journeyed from Bethel . When they were still some distance from Ephrath , Rachel went into labor , and she had hard labor .
verses.preview_html: Gen 35:16 Then they journeyed from Bethel. When they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel went into labor, and she had hard labor.

verses.esv_text: Gen 35:17 And when her labor was at its hardest , the midwife said to her, “Do not fear , for you have another son .”
verses.preview_html: Gen 35:17 And when her labor was at its hardest, the midwife said to her, “Do not fear, for you have another son.”

verses.esv_text: Gen 49:7 Cursed be their anger , for it is fierce , and their wrath , for it is cruel ! I will divide them in Jacob and scatter them in Israel .
verses.preview_html:
Gen 49:7 Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce,
and their wrath, for it is cruel!
I will divide them in Jacob
and scatter them in Israel.

verses.esv_text: Isa 8:21 They will pass through the land, greatly distressed and hungry . And when they are hungry , they will be enraged and will speak contemptuously against their king and their God , and turn their faces upward .
verses.preview_html: Isa 8:21 They will pass through the land, greatly distressed and hungry. And when they are hungry, they will be enraged and will speak contemptuously against their king and their God, and turn their faces upward.

verses.esv_text: Jer 17:23 Yet they did not listen or incline their ear , but stiffened their neck , that they might not hear and receive instruction .
verses.preview_html: Jer 17:23 Yet they did not listen or incline their ear, but stiffened their neck, that they might not hear and receive instruction.

verses.esv_text: Jer 19:15 “ Thus says the Lord of hosts , the God of Israel , behold , I am bringing upon this city and upon all its towns all the disaster that I have pronounced against it, because they have stiffened their neck , refusing to hear my words .”
verses.preview_html: Jer 19:15 “Thus says the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, behold, I am bringing upon this city and upon all its towns all the disaster that I have pronounced against it, because they have stiffened their neck, refusing to hear my words.”

verses.esv_text: Jer 7:26 Yet they did not listen to me or incline their ear , but stiffened their neck . They did worse than their fathers .
verses.preview_html: Jer 7:26 Yet they did not listen to me or incline their ear, but stiffened their neck. They did worse than their fathers.

verses.esv_text: Job 9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength — who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded ?—
verses.preview_html:
Job 9:4 He is wise in heart and mighty in strength—
who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded?—

verses.esv_text: Neh 9:16 “But they and our fathers acted presumptuously and stiffened their neck and did not obey your commandments .
verses.preview_html: Neh 9:16 “But they and our fathers acted presumptuously and stiffened their neck and did not obey your commandments.

verses.esv_text: Neh 9:17 They refused to obey and were not mindful of the wonders that you performed among them, but they stiffened their neck and appointed a leader to return to their slavery in Egypt . But you are a God ready to forgive , gracious and merciful , slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love , and did not forsake them .
verses.preview_html: Neh 9:17 They refused to obey and were not mindful of the wonders that you performed among them, but they stiffened their neck and appointed a leader to return to their slavery in Egypt. But you are a God ready to forgive, gracious and merciful, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love, and did not forsake them.

verses.esv_text: Neh 9:29 And you warned them in order to turn them back to your law . Yet they acted presumptuously and did not obey your commandments , but sinned against your rules , which if a person does them, he shall live by them, and they turned a stubborn shoulder and stiffened their neck and would not obey .
verses.preview_html: Neh 9:29 And you warned them in order to turn them back to your law. Yet they acted presumptuously and did not obey your commandments, but sinned against your rules, which if a person does them, he shall live by them, and they turned a stubborn shoulder and stiffened their neck and would not obey.

verses.esv_text: Pro 28:14 Blessed is the one who fears the Lord always , but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity .
verses.preview_html:
Pro 28:14 Blessed is the one who fears the Lord always,
but whoever hardens his heart will fall into calamity.

verses.esv_text: Pro 29:1 He who is often reproved , yet stiffens his neck , will suddenly be broken beyond healing .
verses.preview_html:
Pro 29:1 He who is often reproved, yet stiffens his neck,
will suddenly be broken beyond healing.

verses.esv_text: Psa 95:8 do not harden your hearts , as at Meribah , as on the day at Massah in the wilderness ,
verses.preview_html:
Psa 95:8 do not harden your hearts, as at Meribah,
as on the day at Massah in the wilderness,

terms.medium_def:
A location first mentioned at Jos.19.20; 
referred to as Kishion (קִשְׁיוֹן), or Kedesh (קֶ֫דֶשׁ). § a town on the boundary of Issachar allotted to the Gershonite Levites

1) to, toward, unto (of motion)
2) into (limit is actually entered)
2a) in among
3) toward (of direction, not necessarily physical motion)
4) against (motion or direction of a hostile character)
5) in addition to, to
6) concerning, in regard to, in reference to, on account of
7) according to (rule or standard)
8) at, by, against (of one's presence)
9) in between, in within, to within, unto (idea of motion to)

A man from the tribe of Simeon(?) living at the time of Divided Monarchy, only mentioned at 1Ch.4.36; 
only referred to as Elioenai (אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי).

A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Ezr.10.22; 
only referred to as Elioenai (אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי).

A man living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Ezr.10.27; 
only referred to as Elioenai (אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי).

A priest living at the time of Exile and Return, only mentioned at Neh.12.41; 
only referred to as Elioenai (אֶלְיוֹעֵינַי).
